revision.text: {{{1|Names for footnotes and groups must follow these rules:}}} * Names are case-sensitive. Please do not use raNdOM capitalization. * Names {{strong|must not}} be purely numeric; the software will accept something like ":31337" (which is punctuation plus a number), but it will ignore "31337" (purely numeric). * Names should have semantic value, so that they can be more easily distinguished from each other by human editors who are looking at the wikitext. This means that ref names like "Nguyen 2010" are preferred to names like ":31337". * Names must be unique. You may not use the same name to define different groups or footnotes. Try to avoid picking a name that someone else is likely to choose for a new citation, such as ":0" or "NYT". * Please consider keeping reference names short, simple, and restricted to the standard [[English alphabet]] and numerals. If spaces are used, the following technical restrictions become relevant: ** Quotation marks are preferred but optional if the only characters used are letters A–Z, a–z, digits 0–9, and the symbols !$%&()*,-.:;<@[]^_`{|}~. That is, all printable ASCII characters except #"'/=<>?\ and space. ** Inclusion of any other characters, {{em|including spaces}}, requires that the reference name be enclosed in quotes; for example, name="John Smith". But quote-enclosed reference names may not include a less-than sign (<) or a double straight quote symbol ("), which may however be included by [[Escape sequence|escaping]] as < and " respectively.{{#if: {{{2|}}}| {{{2}}}}} ** The quote marks must be the standard, straight, double quotation marks ("); curly or other quotes will be parsed as part of the reference name. * You may optionally provide reference names even when the reference name is not required. This makes later re-use of the sourced reference easier. {{documentation}}

{{{1|Names for footnotes and groups must follow these rules:}}} * Names are case-sensitive. Please do not use raNdOM capitalization. * Names {{strong|must not}} be purely numeric; the software will accept something like ":31337" (which is punctuation plus a number), but it will ignore "31337" (purely numeric). * Names should have semantic value, so that they can be more easily distinguished from each other by human editors who are looking at the wikitext. This means that ref names like "Nguyen 2010" are preferred to names like ":31337". * Names must be unique. You may not use the same name to define different groups or footnotes. Try to avoid picking a name that someone else is likely to choose for a new citation, such as ":0" or "NYT". * Please consider keeping reference names short, simple, and restricted to the standard [[English alphabet]] and numerals. If spaces are used, the following technical restrictions become relevant: ** Quotation marks are preferred but optional if the only characters used are letters A–Z, a–z, digits 0–9, and the symbols !$%&()*,-.:;<@[]^_`{|}~. That is, all printable ASCII characters except #"'/=<>?\ and space. ** Inclusion of any other characters, {{em|including spaces}}, requires that the reference name be enclosed in quotes; for example, name="John Smith". But quote-enclosed reference names may not include a less-than sign (<) or a double straight quote symbol ("), which may however be included by [[Escape sequence|escaping]] as < and " respectively.{{#if: {{{2|}}}| {{{2}}}}} ** The quote marks must be the standard, straight, double quotation marks ("); curly or other quotes will be parsed as part of the reference name. * You may optionally provide reference names even when the reference name is not required. This makes later re-use of the sourced reference easier. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: /* top */ missing commas change definition of “however” to nonsense
revision.text: {{{1|Names for footnotes and groups must follow these rules:}}} * Names are case-sensitive. Please do not use raNdOM capitalization. * Names {{strong|must not}} be purely numeric; the software will accept something like ":31337" (which is punctuation plus a number), but it will ignore "31337" (purely numeric). * Names should have semantic value, so that they can be more easily distinguished from each other by human editors who are looking at the wikitext. This means that ref names like "Nguyen 2010" are preferred to names like ":31337". * Names must be unique. You may not use the same name to define different groups or footnotes. Try to avoid picking a name that someone else is likely to choose for a new citation, such as ":0" or "NYT". * Please consider keeping reference names short, simple, and restricted to the standard [[English alphabet]] and numerals. If spaces are used, the following technical restrictions become relevant: ** Quotation marks are preferred but optional if the only characters used are letters A–Z, a–z, digits 0–9, and the symbols !$%&()*,-.:;<@[]^_`{|}~. That is, all printable ASCII characters except #"'/=>?\ and space. ** Inclusion of any other characters, {{em|including spaces}}, requires that the reference name be enclosed in quotes; for example, name="John Smith". But quote-enclosed reference names may not include a less-than sign (<) or a double straight quote symbol ("), which may, however, be included by [[Escape sequence|escaping]] as < and " respectively.{{#if: {{{2|}}}| {{{2}}}}} ** The quote marks must be the standard, straight, double quotation marks ("); curly or other quotes will be parsed as part of the reference name. * You may optionally provide reference names even when the reference name is not required. This makes later re-use of the sourced reference easier. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: /* top */ missing commas change definition of “however” to nonsense
revision.text: {{{1|Names for footnotes and groups must follow these rules:}}} * Names are case-sensitive. Please do not use raNdOM capitalization. * Names {{strong|must not}} be purely numeric; the software will accept something like ":31337" (which is punctuation plus a number), but it will ignore "31337" (purely numeric). * Names should have semantic value, so that they can be more easily distinguished from each other by human editors who are looking at the wikitext. This means that ref names like "Nguyen 2010" are preferred to names like ":31337". * Names must be unique. You may not use the same name to define different groups or footnotes. Try to avoid picking a name that someone else is likely to choose for a new citation, such as ":0" or "NYT". * Please consider keeping reference names short, simple, and restricted to the standard [[English alphabet]] and numerals. If spaces are used, the following technical restrictions become relevant: ** Quotation marks are preferred but optional if the only characters used are letters A–Z, a–z, digits 0–9, and the symbols !$%&()*,-.:;<@[]^_`{|}~. That is, all printable ASCII characters except #"'/=>?\ and space. ** Inclusion of any other characters, {{em|including spaces}}, requires that the reference name be enclosed in quotes; for example, name="John Smith". But quote-enclosed reference names may not include a less-than sign (<) or a double straight quote symbol ("), which may, however, be included by [[Escape sequence|escaping]] as < and " respectively.{{#if: {{{2|}}}| {{{2}}}}} ** The quote marks must be the standard, straight, double quotation marks ("); curly or other quotes will be parsed as part of the reference name. * You may optionally provide reference names even when the reference name is not required. This makes later re-use of the sourced reference easier. {{documentation}}

{{{1|Names for footnotes and groups must follow these rules:}}} * Names are case-sensitive. Please do not use raNdOM capitalization. * Names {{strong|must not}} be purely numeric; the software will accept something like ":31337" (which is punctuation plus a number), but it will ignore "31337" (purely numeric). * Names should have semantic value, so that they can be more easily distinguished from each other by human editors who are looking at the wikitext. This means that ref names like "Nguyen 2010" are preferred to names like ":31337". * Names must be unique. You may not use the same name to define different groups or footnotes. Try to avoid picking a name that someone else is likely to choose for a new citation, such as ":0" or "NYT". * Please consider keeping reference names short, simple, and restricted to the standard [[English alphabet]] and numerals. If spaces are used, the following technical restrictions become relevant: ** Quotation marks are preferred but optional if the only characters used are letters A–Z, a–z, digits 0–9, and the symbols !$%&()*,-.:;<@[]^_`{|}~. That is, all printable ASCII characters except #"'/=>?\ and space. ** Inclusion of any other characters, {{em|including spaces}}, requires that the reference name be enclosed in quotes; for example, name="John Smith". But quote-enclosed reference names may not include a less-than sign (/code>) or a double straight quote symbol ("), which may, however, be included by [[Escape sequence|escaping]] as < and " respectively.{{#if: {{{2|}}}| {{{2}}}}} ** The quote marks must be the standard, straight, double quotation marks ("); curly or other quotes will be parsed as part of the reference name. * You may optionally provide reference names even when the reference name is not required. This makes later re-use of the sourced reference easier. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{{1|Names for footnotes and groups must follow these rules:}}} * Names are case-sensitive. Please do not use raNdOM capitalization. * Names {{strong|must not}} be purely numeric; the software will accept something like ":31337" (which is punctuation plus a number), but it will ignore "31337" (purely numeric). * Names should have semantic value, so that they can be more easily distinguished from each other by human editors who are looking at the wikitext. This means that ref names like "Nguyen 2010" are preferred to names like ":31337". * Names must be unique. You may not use the same name to define different groups or footnotes. Try to avoid picking a name that someone else is likely to choose for a new citation, such as ":0" or "NYT". * Please consider keeping reference names short, simple, and restricted to the standard [[English alphabet]] and numerals. If spaces are used, the following technical restrictions become relevant: ** Quotation marks are preferred but optional if the only characters used are letters A–Z, a–z, digits 0–9, and the symbols !$%&()*,-.:;<@[]^_`{|}~. That is, all printable ASCII characters except #"'/=>?\ and space. ** Inclusion of any other characters, {{em|including spaces}}, requires that the reference name be enclosed in quotes; for example, name="John Smith". But quote-enclosed reference names may not include a less-than sign (/code>) or a double straight quote symbol ("), which may, however, be included by [[:en:Escape sequence|escaping]] as < and " respectively.{{#if: {{{2|}}}| {{{2}}}}} ** The quote marks must be the standard, straight, double quotation marks ("); curly or other quotes will be parsed as part of the reference name. * You may optionally provide reference names even when the reference name is not required. This makes later re-use of the sourced reference easier. {{documentation}}

revision.text: {{{1|Names for footnotes and groups must follow these rules:}}} * Names are case-sensitive. Please do not use raNdOM capitalization. * Names {{strong|must not}} be purely numeric; the software will accept something like ":31337" (which is punctuation plus a number), but it will ignore "31337" (purely numeric). * Names should have semantic value, so that they can be more easily distinguished from each other by human editors who are looking at the wikitext. This means that ref names like "Nguyen 2010" are preferred to names like ":31337". * Names must be unique. You may not use the same name to define different groups or footnotes. Try to avoid picking a name that someone else is likely to choose for a new citation, such as ":0" or "NYT". * Please consider keeping reference names short, simple, and restricted to the standard [[:en:English alphabet|English alphabet]] and numerals. If spaces are used, the following technical restrictions become relevant: ** Quotation marks are preferred but optional if the only characters used are letters A–Z, a–z, digits 0–9, and the symbols !$%&()*,-.:;<@[]^_`{|}~. That is, all printable ASCII characters except #"'/=>?\ and space. ** Inclusion of any other characters, {{em|including spaces}}, requires that the reference name be enclosed in quotes; for example, name="John Smith". But quote-enclosed reference names may not include a less-than sign (/code>) or a double straight quote symbol ("), which may, however, be included by [[:en:Escape sequence|escaping]] as < and " respectively.{{#if: {{{2|}}}| {{{2}}}}} ** The quote marks must be the standard, straight, double quotation marks ("); curly or other quotes will be parsed as part of the reference name. * You may optionally provide reference names even when the reference name is not required. This makes later re-use of the sourced reference easier. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{{1|Names for footnotes and groups must follow these rules:}}} * Names are case-sensitive. Please do not use raNdOM capitalization. * Names {{strong|must not}} be purely numeric; the software will accept something like ":31337" (which is punctuation plus a number), but it will ignore "31337" (purely numeric). * Names should have semantic value, so that they can be more easily distinguished from each other by human editors who are looking at the wikitext. This means that ref names like "Nguyen 2010" are preferred to names like ":31337". * Names must be unique. You may not use the same name to define different groups or footnotes. Try to avoid picking a name that someone else is likely to choose for a new citation, such as ":0" or "NYT". * Please consider keeping reference names short, simple, and restricted to the standard [[:en:English alphabet|English alphabet]] and numerals. If spaces are used, the following technical restrictions become relevant: ** Quotation marks are preferred but optional if the only characters used are letters A–Z, a–z, digits 0–9, and the symbols !$%&()*,-.:;<@[]^_`{|}~. That is, all printable ASCII characters except #"'/=>?\ and space. ** Inclusion of any other characters, {{em|including spaces}}, requires that the reference name be enclosed in quotes; for example, name="John Smith". But quote-enclosed reference names may not include a less-than sign (/code>) or a double straight quote symbol ("), which may, however, be included by [[:en:Escape sequence|escaping]] as < and " respectively.{{#if: {{{2|}}}| {{{2}}}}} ** The quote marks must be the standard, straight, double quotation marks ("); curly or other quotes will be parsed as part of the reference name. * You may optionally provide reference names even when the reference name is not required. This makes later re-use of the sourced reference easier. {{documentation}} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{{1|Names for footnotes and groups must follow these rules:}}} * Names are case-sensitive. Please do not use raNdOM capitalization. * Names {{strong|must not}} be purely numeric; the software will accept something like ":31337" (which is punctuation plus a number), but it will ignore "31337" (purely numeric). * Names should have semantic value, so that they can be more easily distinguished from each other by human editors who are looking at the wikitext. This means that ref names like "Nguyen 2010" are preferred to names like ":31337". * Names must be unique. You may not use the same name to define different groups or footnotes. Try to avoid picking a name that someone else is likely to choose for a new citation, such as ":0" or "NYT". * Please consider keeping reference names short, simple, and restricted to the standard [[:en:English alphabet|English alphabet]] and numerals. If spaces are used, the following technical restrictions become relevant: ** Quotation marks are preferred but optional if the only characters used are letters A–Z, a–z, digits 0–9, and the symbols !$%&()*,-.:;<@[]^_`{|}~. That is, all printable ASCII characters except #"'/=>?\ and space. ** Inclusion of any other characters, {{em|including spaces}}, requires that the reference name be enclosed in quotes; for example, name="John Smith". But quote-enclosed reference names may not include a less-than sign (/code>) or a double straight quote symbol ("), which may, however, be included by [[:en:Escape sequence|escaping]] as < and " respectively.{{#if: {{{2|}}}| {{{2}}}}} ** The quote marks must be the standard, straight, double quotation marks ("); curly or other quotes will be parsed as part of the reference name. * You may optionally provide reference names even when the reference name is not required. This makes later re-use of the sourced reference easier. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{{1|Names for footnotes and groups must follow these rules:}}} * Names are case-sensitive. Please do not use raNdOM capitalization. * Names {{strong|must not}} be purely numeric; the software will accept something like ":31337" (which is punctuation plus a number), but it will ignore "31337" (purely numeric). * Names should have semantic value, so that they can be more easily distinguished from each other by human editors who are looking at the wikitext. This means that ref names like "Nguyen 2010" are preferred to names like ":31337". * Names must be unique. You may not use the same name to define different groups or footnotes. Try to avoid picking a name that someone else is likely to choose for a new citation, such as ":0" or "NYT". * Please consider keeping reference names short, simple, and restricted to the standard [[:en:English alphabet|English alphabet]] and numerals. If spaces are used, the following technical restrictions become relevant: ** Quotation marks are preferred but optional if the only characters used are letters A–Z, a–z, digits 0–9, and the symbols !$%&()*,-.:;<@[]^_`{|}~. That is, all printable ASCII characters except #"'/=>?\ and space. ** Inclusion of any other characters, {{em|including spaces}}, requires that the reference name be enclosed in quotes; for example, name="John Smith". But quote-enclosed reference names may not include a less-than sign (/code>) or a double straight quote symbol ("), which may, however, be included by [[:en:Escape sequence|escaping]] as < and " respectively.{{#if: {{{2|}}}| {{{2}}}}} ** The quote marks must be the standard, straight, double quotation marks ("); curly or other quotes will be parsed as part of the reference name. * You may optionally provide reference names even when the reference name is not required. This makes later re-use of the sourced reference easier. {{documentation}} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23573030467|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{{1|Names for footnotes and groups must follow these rules:}}} * Names are case-sensitive. Please do not use raNdOM capitalization. * Names {{strong|must not}} be purely numeric; the software will accept something like ":31337" (which is punctuation plus a number), but it will ignore "31337" (purely numeric). * Names should have semantic value, so that they can be more easily distinguished from each other by human editors who are looking at the wikitext. This means that ref names like "Nguyen 2010" are preferred to names like ":31337". * Names must be unique. You may not use the same name to define different groups or footnotes. Try to avoid picking a name that someone else is likely to choose for a new citation, such as ":0" or "NYT". * Please consider keeping reference names short, simple, and restricted to the standard [[:en:English alphabet|English alphabet]] and numerals. If spaces are used, the following technical restrictions become relevant: ** Quotation marks are preferred but optional if the only characters used are letters A–Z, a–z, digits 0–9, and the symbols !$%&()*,-.:;<@[]^_`{|}~. That is, all printable ASCII characters except #"'/=>?\ and space. ** Inclusion of any other characters, {{em|including spaces}}, requires that the reference name be enclosed in quotes; for example, name="John Smith". But quote-enclosed reference names may not include a less-than sign (/code>) or a double straight quote symbol ("), which may, however, be included by [[:en:Escape sequence|escaping]] as < and " respectively.{{#if: {{{2|}}}| {{{2}}}}} ** The quote marks must be the standard, straight, double quotation marks ("); curly or other quotes will be parsed as part of the reference name. * You may optionally provide reference names even when the reference name is not required. This makes later re-use of the sourced reference easier. {{documentation}} [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

{{{1|Names for footnotes and groups must follow these rules:}}} * Names are case-sensitive. Please do not use raNdOM capitalization. * Names {{strong|must not}} be purely numeric; the software will accept something like ":31337" (which is punctuation plus a number), but it will ignore "31337" (purely numeric). * Names should have semantic value, so that they can be more easily distinguished from each other by human editors who are looking at the wikitext. This means that ref names like "Nguyen 2010" are preferred to names like ":31337". * Names must be unique. You may not use the same name to define different groups or footnotes. Try to avoid picking a name that someone else is likely to choose for a new citation, such as ":0" or "NYT". * Please consider keeping reference names short, simple, and restricted to the standard [[:en:English alphabet|English alphabet]] and numerals. If spaces are used, the following technical restrictions become relevant: ** Quotation marks are preferred but optional if the only characters used are letters A–Z, a–z, digits 0–9, and the symbols !$%&()*,-.:;<@[]^_`{|}~. That is, all printable ASCII characters except #"'/=>?\ and space. ** Inclusion of any other characters, {{em|including spaces}}, requires that the reference name be enclosed in quotes; for example, name="John Smith". But quote-enclosed reference names may not include a less-than sign (/code>) or a double straight quote symbol ("), which may, however, be included by [[:en:Escape sequence|escaping]] as < and " respectively.{{#if: {{{2|}}}| {{{2}}}}} ** The quote marks must be the standard, straight, double quotation marks ("); curly or other quotes will be parsed as part of the reference name. * You may optionally provide reference names even when the reference name is not required. This makes later re-use of the sourced reference easier. {{documentation}} [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
